description: This figure presents nanoindentation results for Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels with varying Al contents (15Al, 17Al, and 19Al) under optimized aging conditions. Panel (a) displays nanoindentation load-displacement curves showing the 15Al alloy aged at 600°C for 6 h exhibits an average hardness (H_Ave) of 11.7 ± 0.4 GPa, the 17Al alloy aged at 575°C for 6 h shows 11.1 ± 0.6 GPa, and the 19Al alloy aged at 600°C for 10 h shows 10.4 ± 1.3 GPa. The error bars and curve spreads indicate increasing data scatter with higher Al content. Panels (b) show secondary electron (SE) images of the indentation arrays with corresponding hardness values (in GPa) labeled for each indent. The 15Al sample shows relatively uniform hardness values ranging from 10.9 to 12.4 GPa, while the 19Al sample exhibits greater variation (8.0 to 12.9 GPa), visually confirming the increased standard deviation. According to the caption and text, these measurements were taken from areas avoiding FCC Cu-rich and BCC phases to determine the intrinsic hardness of β-Mn precipitates. The results demonstrate that although increasing Al content slightly enhances the intrinsic hardness of β-Mn through solid-solution strengthening (from 12.1 GPa in 15Al to 12.5 GPa in 19Al), the overall micro-hardness decreases due to the increased volume fraction of softer FCC Cu-rich and BCC phases at β-Mn interfaces.
